name: Indra Jatra
description: Eight-day festival honoring Indra, the king of heaven. Features the living goddess Kumari's chariot procession through Kathmandu, masked Lakhe dances, and the display of Akash Bhairav.

name: Bisket Jatra
description: Nepali New Year festival in Bhaktapur. A massive chariot carrying Bhairav is pulled through the streets. Includes the famous tongue-piercing ritual and a tug-of-war with the chariot.

name: Dashain
description: The longest and most important Hindu festival in Nepal. Celebrates the goddess Durga's victory over the buffalo demon Mahishasura. Involves animal sacrifices, tika, and jamara.

name: Tihar
description: Five-day festival of lights honoring Yama (god of death), crows, dogs, cows, and brothers. Includes Laxmi Puja with oil lamps and the Newar New Year (Mha Puja).

name: Gorkha Unification Campaign
description: Prithvi Narayan Shah's military campaign to unify the small kingdoms of Nepal into a single nation. Began with the siege of the Kathmandu Valley.

name: 1934 Bihar-Nepal Earthquake
description: Massive 8.0 magnitude earthquake that devastated the Kathmandu Valley. Destroyed Dharahara tower, damaged Bhaktapur Durbar Square, and killed over 8500 people in Nepal.

name: 2015 Gorkha Earthquake
description: 7.8 magnitude earthquake centered in Gorkha district. Destroyed or damaged 750000 structures including Dharahara, portions of all three Durbar Squares, and hundreds of heritage temples.

name: Rato Machindranath Jatra
description: Longest chariot festival in Patan. The red-faced deity Machindranath is pulled through Patan's streets in a towering chariot over several weeks to ensure good monsoon rains.

name: Ghode Jatra
description: Horse racing festival held at Tundikhel in Kathmandu. Legend says galloping horses keep a demon buried beneath the ground. Observed by the Nepal Army with horse races and acrobatics.

name: Mha Puja
description: Newar New Year self-worship ceremony. Participants sit in a mandala circle and perform rituals to purify and honor the self. Marks Nepal Sambat New Year.
